page: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an MS family virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor running at 2.7 GHz, the system features 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 cache. Hardware-level capabilities include Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX and AVX512F), AES cryptography, and Intel Virtualization Technology (VMX). The instance is bundled with 2097 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 10% tier, alongside poor single-threaded compression and decompression rates. Conversely, multi-threaded performance is significantly stronger. The system achieves average ratings in multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests, and its multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is rated in the top 25% at 186.92 GB/sec. Memory performance is robust, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 210.73 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% tier, while small-block cached read and large-block write bandwidth remain average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M64ls offers high resource density, combining substantial memory capacity and local storage on a dedicated hypervisor allocation. However, the lower single-core performance suggests it is less suitable for latency-sensitive, single-threaded applications. It is best utilized for highly parallelized workloads, such as database operations, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that can leverage the 512 GB memory footprint and 64 vCPUs.
description: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an x86_64 virtual machine from the MS family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, driven by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor at 2.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32 physical cores, a 38 MB L3 cache, and 2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks highlight a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression are weak, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-threaded operations and large-block memory read bandwidth perform well, with the latter reaching 210.73 GB/sec. This resource profile offers high memory and core density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads. The M64ls is highly suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an MS family virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2097 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an MS family VM with 64 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2097 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, half a terabyte of memory, and robust multi-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8280M at 2.7 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-threaded performance and large-block memory read bandwidth of 210.73 GB/sec
Tradeoff of lower single-core performance compared to multi-core execution
Best suited for database operations, parallel data processing, and memory-intensive applications
